channel.title: AI News — A Guide to Cloud & AI
channel.description: Daily AI news digest — curated headlines, summaries and trends

item.title: Factory hits $1.5B valuation to build AI coding for enterprises
item.description: Factory, a startup focused on AI coding for enterprises, has achieved a $1.5 billion valuation after raising $150 million led by Khosla Ventures. This funding will help accelerate its growth in the competitive AI market.

item.title: Anthropic won't own MCP 'design flaw' putting 200K servers at risk, researcher says
item.description: A design flaw in Anthropic's Model Context Protocol (MCP) may expose 200,000 servers to security risks, raising concerns among researchers about the implications for data security. The distinction between a flaw and expected behavior complicates accountability.

item.title: Luma launches AI-powered production studio with faith-focused Wonder Project
item.description: Luma has launched an AI-powered production studio, debuting with a project titled the Wonder Project, focused on Moses and featuring Academy Award-winner Ben Kingsley. The project will be available on Prime Video this spring.

item.title: Mozilla throws Thunderbolt at enterprise AI providers
item.description: Mozilla has introduced an open-source alternative to enterprise AI platforms, challenging major players like OpenAI and Microsoft by emphasizing data privacy. This move aims to attract businesses seeking more secure AI solutions.

item.title: I tried the new Gemini app for Mac - and it's better than the website in one big way
item.description: The new Gemini app for Mac offers enhanced functionality compared to its website version, allowing users to access and analyze content from any shared window. This feature is expected to improve user experience significantly.

item.title: NodeWeaver says its perpetual licensing beats VMware’s perpetual price hikes
item.description: NodeWeaver is positioning itself as a cost-effective alternative to VMware, especially for customers facing price hikes. The company claims its perpetual licensing model can run efficiently in diverse edge locations.

item.title: Upscale AI in talks to raise at $2B valuation, says report
item.description: Upscale AI is reportedly in talks to raise funds at a $2 billion valuation, marking its third funding round within seven months of launching. This reflects strong investor confidence in AI infrastructure.

item.title: Physical Intelligence, a hot robotics startup, says its new robot brain can figure out tasks it was never taught
item.description: Physical Intelligence has developed a new robot brain, π0.7, which can learn to perform tasks it wasn't explicitly taught. This advancement is seen as a significant step towards creating general-purpose robots.

item.title: Anthropic squeezes enterprises by ejecting bundled tokens from seat deal
item.description: Anthropic has changed its pricing model for enterprise customers, moving them to a metered pricing plan upon contract renewal, which may lead to increased costs for users of its Claude AI system.

item.title: Google's Pixel 10 is now 30% off on Amazon for a limited time
item.description: Amazon is offering a limited-time discount on Google's Pixel 10, reducing its price to $549. The phone is noted for its flagship performance and excellent camera system.

item.title: OpenAI takes aim at Anthropic with beefed-up Codex that gives it more power over your desktop
item.description: OpenAI has revamped its Codex tool, enhancing its capabilities to provide more control over desktop applications, posing a challenge to competitors like Anthropic.

item.title: Loud, power hungry - opposition grows to datacenters as Maine passes bit barn ban
item.description: Maine has enacted a ban on new datacenters due to public opposition, highlighting concerns over their environmental impact and energy consumption.

item.title: How Google's updated AI Mode will ease your tab clutter when you search
item.description: Google's updated AI Mode allows users to view search results alongside their search window, improving the search experience and reducing tab clutter.

item.title: Why this MagSafe battery pack is our readers' favorite model right now - especially at its price
item.description: The Torras MiniMag Power Bank has become a favorite among readers for its sleek design and affordability, standing out in the crowded battery pack market.

item.title: Anthropic CPO leaves Figma’s board after reports he will offer a competing product
item.description: Anthropic's Chief Product Officer has left Figma's board amid speculation of launching a competing design tool, raising concerns about AI's dominance in software.

item.title: T-Mobile will give you a Google Pixel 10a for free - plus an extra gift
item.description: T-Mobile is offering the Google Pixel 10a for free, along with a complimentary pair of Pixel Buds 2a, as part of a promotional campaign.

item.title: North Korea targets macOS users in latest heist
item.description: North Korean hackers are targeting macOS users through social engineering tactics, tricking them into installing malware disguised as a Zoom update.

item.title: Manage agent skills with GitHub CLI
item.description: GitHub has introduced a new command, 'gh skill', to help developers manage AI agent skills, enhancing their workflow with coding agents.

item.title: Brussels tells Google to hand rivals its search crown jewels as privacy row brews
item.description: Brussels has instructed Google to share its search data with rivals, as part of efforts to enforce competition regulations in the tech sector.

item.title: Make crappy moves around AI and face voter backlash, govts warned
item.description: The UK government is warned of potential voter backlash against AI initiatives unless they demonstrate clear benefits for the public.

item.title: Runway CEO says AI could help Hollywood make 50 films instead of one $100M blockbuster
item.description: Runway's CEO suggests that AI could enable Hollywood to produce 50 films for the cost of one blockbuster, potentially increasing the chances of creating hits. This approach could revolutionize film production by emphasizing quantity over high-budget singular projects.

item.title: Tidal vs. Qobuz: I tried both hi-res streaming services, and they couldn't be more different
item.description: A comparison of Tidal and Qobuz reveals that while both offer high-resolution audio streaming, their features cater to different user preferences. The choice between them ultimately depends on individual listening needs and priorities.

item.title: OpenAI’s big Codex update is a direct shot at Claude Code
item.description: OpenAI has updated its Codex system with new features that enhance coding capabilities, including using local computers and generating images. This update intensifies competition with Anthropic's Claude Code, as both companies strive for dominance in AI coding.

item.title: Introducing GPT-Rosalind for life sciences research
item.description: OpenAI has launched GPT-Rosalind, a new model designed to enhance research in life sciences, particularly in drug discovery and genomics. This model aims to streamline scientific workflows and improve research outcomes.

item.title: MixAtlas: Uncertainty-aware Data Mixture Optimization for Multimodal LLM Midtraining
item.description: The paper discusses MixAtlas, a new framework for optimizing data mixtures in multimodal training, which could enhance sample efficiency and generalization. This research addresses a gap in current multimodal pretraining methods.

item.title: Introducing Claude Opus 4.7
item.description: Claude Opus 4.7 has been introduced, although specific details about its features and improvements were not provided. This update continues the evolution of Anthropic's AI offerings.

item.title: Smashing Security podcast #463: This AI company leaked its own code. It’s also built something terrifying
item.description: A hacking group claims to have breached Venice's flood defense system and is selling access, while Anthropic accidentally leaked its own source code. This incident raises concerns about cybersecurity in AI companies.

item.title: pytorch-kinematics 0.10.0
item.description: The release of pytorch-kinematics 0.10.0 provides a new implementation for robot kinematics using the PyTorch framework. This update is relevant for developers working in robotics and AI.

item.title: Google One discount takes 50% off YouTube Premium for a year
item.description: Google One is offering a limited-time discount of 50% off YouTube Premium for a year, following recent enhancements to its AI Pro storage. This promotion aims to attract more subscribers to its services.

item.title: langchain-aws 1.4.4
item.description: The langchain-aws 1.4.4 integration package connects AWS with LangChain, facilitating the use of AWS services within LangChain applications. This update is beneficial for developers leveraging both platforms.

item.title: Newsroom PR Aligns Leadership Around LLM Visibility
item.description: Howard Breuer has stepped down as CEO of Newsroom PR to focus on business development, with Brian Skoloff taking over as CEO. This leadership change aims to enhance the firm's visibility in the AI sector.

item.title: langchain-agentkit 0.21.4
item.description: The latest version of langchain-agentkit, 0.21.4, introduces a composable extension framework for LangGraph agents, enhancing their functionality.

item.title: Xiaomi 18 Pro appears in new renders with a dedicated AI button
item.description: New renders of the Xiaomi 18 Pro reveal a dedicated AI button, hinting at advanced AI features in the upcoming smartphone expected in five months.

item.title: Ethereum Finds Its Bullish Catalyst – And It’s Bigger Than Price
item.description: Ethereum's recent price surge above $2,300 is attributed to a unique bullish catalyst identified by XWIN Research Japan, marking a significant moment for the cryptocurrency.

item.title: Samsung Elevates Experiences to Care for Users and Their Families with SmartThings Update
item.description: Samsung's SmartThings update enhances family care features, integrating home security and care for pets, aiming to provide better support for families living apart.

item.title: Boston Dynamics' Robot Dog Can Now Read Gauges, Spot Spills, and Reason
item.description: Boston Dynamics has upgraded its Spot robot dog with Google DeepMind's technology, allowing it to autonomously read gauges and identify spills for industrial inspections.

item.title: Molotov-slinging menace allegedly strikes Tesla shop amid wave of corporate arson attacks
item.description: A Tesla sales office in New Orleans was targeted in an arson attack, prompting investigations by authorities amid a series of corporate arson incidents.

item.title: Google、ショートカットキーで素早く呼び出せる100%ネイティブSwiftでApple Silicon Mac用に開発されたGeminiクライアント「Gemini for Mac」をリリース。
item.description: Google has released a native Gemini client for macOS, allowing users to quickly access the application with a keyboard shortcut, enhancing user convenience.

item.title: ashmatics-tools added to PyPI
item.description: The Ashmatics-tools package has been added to PyPI, providing shared utilities and base classes for Ashmatics Knowledge Base applications.

item.title: Mozilla Localization (L10N): Localizer Spotlight: Baurzhan
item.description: Baurzhan Muftakhidinov, a long-time contributor to Mozilla localization, shares his journey and experiences in enhancing software accessibility for diverse languages.

item.title: curaster 0.6.0
item.description: Curaster 0.6.0 is a library that enhances raster algebra and reprojection using CUDA acceleration, improving performance for developers. This update is significant for those working in graphics and data visualization.

item.title: langchain-agentkit 0.21.3
item.description: Langchain-agentkit 0.21.3 introduces a composable extension framework for LangGraph agents, allowing for more flexible AI agent development. This framework enhances the capabilities of AI applications built on Langchain.

item.title: gobby 0.3.7
item.description: Gobby 0.3.7 is a local-first daemon that integrates various AI coding tools, enabling session tracking and task management across platforms. This tool aims to streamline the coding process for AI developers.

item.title: helm-langchain 0.4.0
item.description: Helm-langchain 0.4.0 provides a governance adapter for Langchain, implementing fail-closed tool calling for AI agents. This update is crucial for ensuring safe and reliable AI tool usage.

item.title: helm-langchain added to PyPI
item.description: Helm-langchain has been added to PyPI, offering a governance adapter for Langchain with fail-closed tool calling. This addition makes it easier for developers to access and implement governance in their AI projects.

item.title: graphifyy 0.4.15
item.description: Graphifyy 0.4.15 is an AI coding assistant that turns various types of files into a queryable knowledge graph. This tool aims to enhance the organization and accessibility of coding resources.

item.title: nteract 2.2.1a202604152158
item.description: Nteract 2.2.1a202604152158 integrates AI into Jupyter notebooks, providing a server for various AI agents. This integration allows users to leverage AI capabilities directly within their notebooks.

item.title: DoorDash PR Head Crashes Out Online Over Backlash To Trump Stunt: 'You Need To Touch Grass'
item.description: The DoorDash PR head faced backlash online after responding to complaints about a controversial Trump stunt, highlighting the challenges of managing public relations in the digital age. This incident underscores the importance of effective communication strategies.

item.title: Zettlab D4 AI NAS
item.description: The Zettlab D4 AI NAS is a versatile four-bay network-attached storage solution designed for 4K media streaming, backups, and personal cloud services, featuring an eight-core CPU and AI productivity tools. This product is significant for users needing robust storage solutions.

item.title: New report claims App Store search suggestions and ads steered users to ‘nudify’ apps
item.description: A new report from the Tech Transparency Project reveals that Apple's search suggestions and ads may inadvertently promote 'nudify' apps, raising concerns about app store safety and user guidance. This finding emphasizes the need for better oversight in app store practices.
